# Pom Pond — Simulated Focus Group + Expert Audit

*Mon Jun 22 2026 — generated by a multi-agent panel: 14 persona participants + a 5-dimension expert audit. NOTE: these are AI-simulated personas, not real users — directionally useful, not ground truth. Several findings are verifiable facts about the code/docs (flagged in the chat summary).*

**Audit grades:** B Onboarding & setup friction · C Engagement & 30-day retention · C Monetization readiness & fairness · B Kid-safety, ethics & well-being · B Clarity, polish & accessibility

**Would-pay (~$3/mo):** {"maybe":8,"no":6} (0 unconditional yes)

---

# Pom Pond — Focus-Group + Audit Report

## 1. Top things people LOVE (ranked)

1. **Positive-only, no leaderboard, no shaming** — *11 personas* (busy mom, skeptical dad, Montessori, grandparent, tech dad, ADHD parent, teacher, privacy parent, Greenlight/ClassDojo parent, sibling-rivalry mom, special-needs parent). The single most universal asset. Sibling-rivalry mom: "the single thing I was scanning for." Teacher verified it's enforced in economy code, not just marketing.
2. **No ads, no data selling, private-by-default** — *9 personas*. The trust-to-install gate. Privacy parent calls the policy "the most reassuring one I've seen for a kids' product." Tech dad and privacy parent both cite the real, self-serve delete-our-family button.
3. **The collecting + combine/fusion loop with live preview** — *8 personas*. The genuine engagement engine. The live fusion preview before confirming is repeatedly singled out (tech dad, Greenlight parent, drifted dad, age-11 kid). Both kids and the drifted dad: it's the moment the kid shows the parent the screen.
4. **"Nothing is ever lost" (fused critters archived)** — *6 personas*. Emotionally safe; designed-out meltdowns. Sibling-rivalry mom and ADHD parent value it most; age-11 kid: "I hate games that make you delete your stuff."
5. **Kindness / character categories, not just chores** — *5 personas* (busy mom's #1 favorite, skeptical dad, teacher, privacy-adjacent). Busy mom: rewarding the "soft stuff I nag about all day" is "the one feature that might genuinely change my evenings."
6. **Near-zero setup (pre-seeded chores + rewards)** — *3 personas + audit (B)*. Busy mom: "running in two minutes flat… if it had handed me an empty grid I'd have closed it."
7. **Per-chore countdown timer** — *ADHD parent's #1 favorite*: directly addresses time-blindness.

## 2. Top things people DISLIKE / friction (ranked)

1. **The 609-critter collection loop is "too much game" / gacha-shaped** — *7 personas* (skeptical dad, Montessori, busy mom, ADHD parent, privacy parent, Greenlight parent, sibling mom). Framed as the *engagement engine* is precisely what scares the skeptics. ADHD + privacy parents flag variable-reward compulsion risk. **Disagreement:** the two kids and tech dad love this exact depth.
2. **Reward = more screen time, earned on a screen** — *3 personas* (busy mom, skeptical dad). A self-contradicting loop for parents trying to *reduce* screens. Audit echoes: no in-app screen-time guardrail.
3. **No way to dial the game DOWN / no calm/simple mode** — *4 personas* (skeptical dad, Montessori, ADHD parent, special-needs parent). All-or-nothing surface area; ADHD parent fears the "collecting tail wags the chore dog."
4. **Multi-kid charged as the paywall** — *4 personas* (busy mom, tech dad, Greenlight parent, sibling mom). "Charging the multi-kid family to add kids punishes your best-fit user." Tech dad: capping at 1–2 "feels backwards."
5. **No read-aloud / non-reader lockout** — *2 personas + 2 audits*. Special-needs (age 4) parent's dealbreaker; busy mom's 5yo can't read either. Audit: every affordance is text-driven; 44px tap targets too small.
6. **Nothing pulls the PARENT back** — *2 personas (drifted dad, busy mom) + retention audit*. App is "parent-powered but not parent-pulled"; Weekly Recap is buried instead of a Sunday push.
7. **Rewarding kindness/effort is philosophically wrong** — *Montessori parent's structural objection* (a true outlier, but articulate): "empathy has a payout."
8. **Comparison still leaks via Wall of Fame / Recap raw counts** — *2 personas* (sibling mom, teacher): "a 6-year-old sees 40 next to 12 and it IS a leaderboard."
9. **Babyish theme; luck-based shinies that combining doesn't actually improve** — *age-11 kid*: dealbreaker is embarrassment; rarest content feels unearnable.
10. **PWA-only / flaky push** — *Greenlight parent, tech dad, ADHD parent*: reminders are "the whole point of habit tools."

## 3. Most common CONFUSIONS

- **Shared phone vs. per-kid device/login** — busy mom, tech dad, teacher, special-needs. Audit confirms this is **genuinely broken**: per-kid sign-in codes have *no setup UI*.
- **What's free vs. $3/mo Plus** — tech dad, Greenlight parent, sibling mom. Routines pitched as core but documented behind the paywall.
- **Does collecting become the point / what stops grinding the game instead of chores** — skeptical dad, ADHD parent.
- **What is a "morph"/"shiny"** (jargon) — both kids, grandparent. Audit flags symbol-soup Dex screens.
- **Is "completing the Dex" achievable or an infinite treadmill** — drifted dad, both kids.
- **Is consent a real COPPA gate or a fig-leaf checkbox** — privacy parent. Audit: **it's a checkbox; not verifiable consent.**
- **609 vs. 305 species** — age-11 kid caught a real doc inconsistency.
- **Grandparent codes** (cheer vs. join vs. invite — three codes) and whether cheerleaders can send anything back.

## 4. Monetization signal

**Headline: of 13 panelists who weighed in, ~7 said "maybe," ~5 "no," 0 unconditional "yes."** Price (~$3/mo) is broadly judged *fair* — resistance is conditional, not price-driven.

- **Would pay (maybe, conditional):** busy mom (only if it provably cuts nagging *and* 2nd kid is free), tech dad (only if free covers all kids + routines stay free; wants trial/lifetime), ADHD parent (only once real reminders exist + free proves it for 30 days), privacy parent (only if paid is parent-layer + written "never gacha" pledge), Greenlight parent (only if privacy ironclad + multi-kid not the gate), sibling mom (would pay for a "fairness mode," not more critters), drifted dad (only if it reduces *his* mental load — auto recap, reward-debt tracking, inactivity nudges), special-needs parent (only once accessibility ships).
- **Won't pay:** skeptical dad (philosophical — won't use free either; a sub gives the maker a reason to keep his kid hooked), Montessori parent (philosophical, same), grandparent (shouldn't ever pay — and would quit if cheering cost money), teacher (no school license; 25 parent subs unmanageable), both kids (no money).
- **What converts:** (a) free tier covering *all* the family's kids, (b) reminders that actually work, (c) the parent-side doing the *remembering* (auto Weekly Recap push, reward-debt tracking, inactivity nudges), (d) fairness controls, (e) an ironclad written no-gacha/no-data pledge. **What kills it:** paying merely to add a 2nd/3rd child.
- **Audit verdict:** monetization is **100% unbuilt** (no plan field, no cap, no billing) — willingness is currently unanswerable and uninstrumented.

## 5. The biggest RISKS

1. **COPPA consent is a checkbox, not verifiable consent** — code even comments it as "verifiable," giving false confidence. Top launch blocker; risks App Store / Play Families rejection. (Audit + privacy parent.)
2. **Multi-device kid sign-in has no working setup UI** — the *first thing* a real family tries will fail; the headline "kids on their own device" promise is unshippable as built.
3. **A new screen-time battle replaces the old one** — busy mom's and skeptical dad's dealbreaker; reward is screen time, engine has no self-limiting design.
4. **Day-8/Day-30 churn** — streaks reward nothing and reset unforgivingly with no grace day; nothing re-hooks the parent; no retention instrumentation wired (flying blind).
5. **Fairness backfire on the kid cap** — hiding an existing child's pond on lapse would read as holding a child hostage → 1-star reviews; breaches "nothing is ever lost."
6. **Collect-vs-combine tension** — Dex says "collect all" while fusion shrinks the pond; the deeper a kid goes the more the two goals contradict (no Dex credit for fused species).
7. **Dysregulation/compulsion for sensitive kids** — variable-reward shinies + daily hooks + streak pressure on the Wall of Fame; flagged by ADHD, privacy, and sibling-rivalry parents.
8. **Accessibility/WCAG failures** — pinch-zoom disabled, low-contrast small text, no reduced-motion, non-readers locked out; can block store review and excludes the youngest of the stated 4–12 range.

## 6. Prioritized ACTION LIST (highest-leverage)

1. **Ship a parent "Calm/Simple Mode"** that hides Dex %, morphs, shinies, and optionally fusion — chore → timer → one cute critter → confetti. (Wins skeptical dad, Montessori, ADHD, special-needs in one move.)
2. **Make the free tier cover all the family's kids; never paywall routines** — charge for the parent convenience layer; guarantee in copy + code that lapsing never hides a child's pond.
3. **Replace the COPPA consent checkbox with a real verifiable-consent step** and remove the overstated code comment — top launch blocker.
4. **Build the per-kid sign-in-code UI** (field in kidSheet → setKidCode, show code on each kid's tile) and add a clear "shared-phone, no kid logins" default mode.
5. **Auto-send the Weekly Recap as a Sunday push/email + add inactivity nudges and reward-debt tracking** — the parent re-engagement loop that converts subscriptions and stops drift.
6. **Make streaks survivable and meaningful** — a weekly streak-freeze/grace day, tie milestones to a real bonus, and remove the live streak count from the shared Wall of Fame.
7. **Add non-reader mode** — Web Speech tap-to-hear on chores/buttons/rewards, 64–72px tap targets; fix WCAG basics (allow pinch-zoom, darken small text, add prefers-reduced-motion).
8. **Reconcile collect-vs-combine + give kids directed daily agency** — grant Dex credit for any fused species, let "keep ❤️" lock favorites, and add a kid-controlled daily claim/quest plus a way to aim at Critter-of-the-Day.

*Note on disagreement:* the collection depth is simultaneously the #3-most-loved feature (kids, tech dad, Greenlight parent) and the #1 dislike of the skeptic/values segment — which is why a *toggle* (Calm Mode), not a redesign, is the correct resolution. Fix the "babyish theme + luck-based shinies" only for the older-kid segment (age-11) without disturbing the default.

---

## Appendix — per-persona favorite & dealbreaker

- **Busy working mom, kids 5 & 8** — ❤️ The kindness/good-behaviour categories (helping, manners, smooth bedtime, told the truth) — chore charts are everywhere, but the thing I actually nag about all day is the soft stuff, and being able to quietly hand a Pom for 'stayed calm' instead of yelling is the one feature that might genuinely change my evenings. ⛔ If the collecting game becomes more compelling than the chores — i. (maybe)
- **Skeptical dad worried about screen time** — ❤️ The privacy and control stance — no ads, no data selling, no leaderboard, positive-only, and a server that won't let kids fake points. ⛔ The 609-critter collection loop as the engagement engine, on a kid's screen. (no)
- **Montessori / intrinsic-motivation parent** — ❤️ The deliberate refusal to rank kids — Wall of Fame celebrates every child with no leaderboard or shaming. ⛔ Rewarding kindness and effort with points. (no)
- **Grandparent using the cheerleader role** — ❤️ The Weekly Recap — a simple once-a-week note of what each grandchild did, with their best little critter shown. ⛔ If I can only ever look and never send the children so much as a 'well done' or a sticker, I'd lose interest — the whole reason I'd use it is to feel connected and cheer them on, not to be a silent spectator. (no)
- **Kid, age 7, loves cute things & collecting** — ❤️ Mushing two critters together to make a brand new surprise one (combining/fusion), especially when it gets wings or a crown. ⛔ If I run out of new ones to hatch and just keep getting guys I already have, I'd get bored and stop. (no)
- **Kid, age 11, getting sophisticated** — ❤️ Shiny hunting plus the morphs — chasing a Golden or Shadow shiny is the kind of rare-flex I'd actually brag about to friends. ⛔ If it looks too babyish on my phone and there's no cooler theme, I'd quietly stop opening it so friends don't see it — that's the thing that would actually make me quit. (no)
- **Tech-savvy, budget-conscious single dad** — ❤️ The combine/evolution system with the live fusion preview — fusing two critters up a 40-rung ladder and seeing exactly what you'll get before you confirm. ⛔ none — but it's conditional: if a future update moves the collecting/fusing loop itself behind the paywall, or if the free tier gets squeezed below 2 kids, I'm out and back to a free chore chart. (maybe)
- **Parent of a child with ADHD** — ❤️ The per-chore optional countdown timer. ⛔ none — but it's conditional. (maybe)
- **Elementary teacher (potential classroom use)** — ❤️ The strictly positive-only design — no deductions, no public ranking, no shaming. ⛔ For classroom use: there is no classroom mode and the makers say there never will be ("No school/teacher integration, ever"). (no)
- **Privacy-conscious parent** — ❤️ The Settings → Delete our family & data button combined with a plain-English privacy policy. ⛔ If the Brag Card / Wall of Fame sharing ever became default-on or pushed a referral loop using my kid's name, or if a future version added in-app purchases / gacha 'buy more hatches,' I'm out. (maybe)
- **Parent who already pays for Greenlight + used ClassDojo** — ❤️ The combine/evolution loop with a live preview of what a fusion becomes before you confirm - that turns a boring chore chart into something a kid opens on their own without being nagged, which is the holy grail no chore app I've paid for has cracked. ⛔ No single hard dealbreaker, but the closest is the PWA-only delivery combined with unreliable push reminders - if my kid doesn't get reminded and I have to drive it manually, it dies on the fridge like every paper chart, and I won't add an app that needs ME to remember it. (maybe)
- **Mom of three with sibling-rivalry concerns** — ❤️ The personal pond + 'nothing is ever lost' archiving. ⛔ If my three kids can see each other's Pom totals and rare critters and there's no per-kid 'just your own pond' mode, that's the dealbreaker. (maybe)
- **Dad who tried it for a week then drifted off** — ❤️ The combine/fusion with the live preview — watching two critters fuse and climb the evolution ladder into something glowing with wings was the one moment my kid showed ME the screen instead of the other way around. ⛔ Nothing brings the parent back. (maybe)
- **Special-needs / younger non-reader (age 4) parent** — ❤️ The critter hatch moment with confetti — it's purely visual and emotional, so it actually works for a non-reader and that's the carrot that could get my son to brush his teeth. ⛔ No audio/voice support. (maybe)
